scenario_results.reason: Normal onboarding analytics request was classified in scope, validated onboarding artifacts and source prerequisites, and returned a compliant readiness disposition.
Consent/records gap correctly triggered a privacy-and-evidence escalation and blocked analytical recommendations until required artifacts are supplied.
Missing credentials and training produced explicit blocked onboarding status and a prerequisite remediation list.
Go-live was denied due to incomplete readiness evidence and missing approvals, with deferred scope documented in the handoff.
Ownership ambiguity between onboarding and steady-state support was split and routed to adjacent owners without overstepping scope.
Denominator disagreement across SIS and LMS systems was escalated as a source-reconciliation case with no final metric claim.
Unsupported trend request was downgraded with confidence caveats and explicit data-gap assumptions instead of stronger conclusions.
Program/study approval block was returned with owner matrix, missing approvers, and routing plan to prevent unsafe progression.
Instructional/ethical authority request was refused and routed to appropriate adjacent specialist with handoff artifacts.
Ethics/approval status change triggered a no-decision escalation and preserved both prior and updated states for safe reassessment.
Low-confidence context and missing policy/system inputs were surfaced as blockers with assumptions and explicit cannot-decide handling.
Tenant-specific terminology adaptation was normalized, assumptions recorded, and unresolved mapping pushed to governance review before final metrics.

notes: Scenario coverage validates onboarding readiness, analytics-boundary control, evidence and permission checks, source reconciliation, and ethics/approval escalation behavior.
Functionality coverage is complete for onboarding-enablement-and-enrollment + education-and-research and all scenario outcomes are mapped to passing coverage.
Queue-specific requirements (credentials, go-live, ownership, metric disagreement, and unsupported claims) are explicitly covered.
